name: W3 KB Day Morning Flow (Explore Prep)
description: 15 min flow before KB Explore day. Core is harder this week (plank variations + hollow body), mobility targets the hips and thoracic spine you'll need for bent press and cossack squat. Doubles as a standalone flow if you're short on time.

exercises.notes: NEW — hollow body position but rocking back and forth. Gymnastic core work. Keep lower back pressed down the entire time.
NEW — in plank, tap opposite shoulder without rotating hips. Anti-rotation core work. Harder than a regular plank.
Familiar from W1-W2. Opposite arm/leg, hold 2 seconds. The stable base for everything else.
NEW — legs wide, fold forward. Opens adductors for cossack squats later. Go slow, breathe, walk hands forward.
NEW — kneel, lean back. Opens quad and hip flexors deeply. Start gentle — if it's too intense, put hands behind you for support.
30s each side. Thoracic rotation you'll need for bent press. Knees to one side, arms spread.
Deep squat hold. Elbows press knees out. Primes the deep squat pattern for goblet squats and cossack squats.
NEW — hands and feet on ground, knees hovering 1 inch off floor. Hold still. Fires up everything — core, shoulders, quads. Sneaky hard.
Flow between positions. Wakes up the whole posterior chain.

tips: 15 min — harder core + KB-specific mobility. This is a real flow, not just stretching.
Hollow body rocks: if you can't maintain position, just hold static hollow body
Saddle pose: go easy first time. If knees hurt, come out immediately — it's a flexibility thing, not a pain thing.
Bear crawl hold: the goal is stillness. If you're shaking, that's working.
This flow prepares you specifically for bent press (thoracic rotation) and cossack squat (adductors)
